sections.about: This metric reports worker fatalities from work-related injury, including contractor/third-party workers where the company’s BRSR definition includes them. It is a Principle 3 Essential Indicator. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Tata improved from 6 in FY23 to 5 in FY24 and 4 in FY25, ranking 2nd in FY25. Jindal showed the strongest trajectory, from 3 to 1 to 0. SAIL reported 10 in FY23, improving to 7 in FY24 and 5 in FY25. JSW improved sharply from 6 in FY23 to 2 in FY24 but worsened to 5 in FY25, equalling SAIL. Worker fatalities are the most serious indicator for integrated steel operations because fatal exposure concentrates in contractor-intensive jobs: logistics, maintenance shutdowns, hot zones, rail/road movement, confined spaces and height work. Tata’s contractor safety risk management, vendor audits, road/rail safety controls and process safety actions are aligned with the right risk areas, but the FY25 count shows residual fatality exposure remains material.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked 2nd at 4 worker fatalities. Jindal reported 0, so percentage gap is not meaningful; the absolute gap is 4 fatalities. Tata improved numerically each year, declining from joint 2nd in FY23 to 3rd in FY24, then recovering to 2nd in FY25 as JSW’s worker fatalities worsened from 2 to 5.
sections.targets: Tata states zero harm as the safety ambition. Jindal states Zero Harm and Zero LTI orientation.
